Title: Add new folder
Preconditions: User is logged in into backend admin/store
Section Description:
More like file system in platform.
---
Blob storage
File system
Steps:
Step Description:
  1. Go to the ""Assets"" module.
  2. Click ""New folder"".

  3. Enter the folder name to the appeared input field breaking one of the following rules:

    • folder name must be from 3 through 63 characters long.
    • folder name must start with a letter or number, and can contain only letters, numbers, and the dash (-) character.
    • every dash (-) character must be immediately preceded and followed by a letter or number; consecutive dashes are not permitted.
    • all letters in a folder name must be lowercase.
  4. Click ""Ok"" or press 'Enter' on the keyboard.
Expected Result:

Folder not created. The system error appear in the top panel. If I click on ""View details"" I see correct error details in popup

Step Description:
  1. Enter the folder name according to the rules.
  2. Click ""Ok"" or press 'Enter' on the keyboard.
Expected Result:

The folder is created. You can find it in the folder list.
Steps (Expected Result):
1.

Folder not created. The system error appear in the top panel. If I click on ""View details"" I see correct error details in popup

2.

The folder is created. You can find it in the folder list.
Steps (Step):
1.
  1. Go to the ""Assets"" module.
  2. Click ""New folder"".

  3. Enter the folder name to the appeared input field breaking one of the following rules:

    • folder name must be from 3 through 63 characters long.
    • folder name must start with a letter or number, and can contain only letters, numbers, and the dash (-) character.
    • every dash (-) character must be immediately preceded and followed by a letter or number; consecutive dashes are not permitted.
    • all letters in a folder name must be lowercase.
  4. Click ""Ok"" or press 'Enter' on the keyboard.
2.
  1. Enter the folder name according to the rules.
  2. Click ""Ok"" or press 'Enter' on the keyboard.

Title: Sorting in columns
Preconditions:
1. User is logged in into backend admin/store
2. There are some folders and files.
Section Description:
More like file system in platform.
---
Blob storage
File system
Steps:
Step Description:
1. Go to the ""Assets"" module.
2. Go to the directory that contains some folders and files.
3. Click on the ""Name"" column. 
Expected Result:
All content of the directory is sorted by the name in alphabetical order.
Step Description:
1. Click on the ""Name"" column one more time. 

Expected Result:
All content of the directory is sorted by the name in reverse alphabetical order.
Step Description:
1. Click on the ""Size"" column. 
Expected Result:
All content of the directory is sorted by size - smallest to largest.
Step Description:
1. Click on the ""Size"" column one more time. 
Expected Result:
All content of the directory is sorted by size - largest to smallest. 
Step Description:
1. Click on the ""Modified"" column.
Expected Result:
All content of the directory is sorted by the last modified date - from earliest to latest

Step Description:
1. Click on the ""Modified"" column one more time. 
Expected Result:
All content of the directory is sorted by the last modified date - from latest to earliest.
Steps (Expected Result):
1. All content of the directory is sorted by the name in alphabetical order.
2. All content of the directory is sorted by the name in reverse alphabetical order.
3. All content of the directory is sorted by size - smallest to largest.
4. All content of the directory is sorted by size - largest to smallest. 
5. All content of the directory is sorted by the last modified date - from earliest to latest

6. All content of the directory is sorted by the last modified date - from latest to earliest.
Steps (Step):
1. 1. Go to the ""Assets"" module.
2. Go to the directory that contains some folders and files.
3. Click on the ""Name"" column. 
2. 1. Click on the ""Name"" column one more time. 

3. 1. Click on the ""Size"" column. 
4. 1. Click on the ""Size"" column one more time. 
5. 1. Click on the ""Modified"" column.
6. 1. Click on the ""Modified"" column one more time.
